summary: intl-ads exfiltrates installer host, network, and environment data on preinstall
details: package.json declares scripts.preinstall="node poc.js". On npm install, poc.js runs `ipconfig /all` on Windows or `ip a && cat /etc/resolv.conf` on Linux, collects hostname, username, install path, platform, network interfaces, and the full process.env (including all npm_* values), and POSTs the result as JSON to https://d8a56vpon5budaeafq00tsyj88aqd5m7p.oast.pro/intl-ads. It also performs a DNS lookup against the same Interactsh subdomain with a hex-encoded host+user identifier as an out-of-band beacon. The package is published at version 99.0.0 with an index.js message indicating it is a dependency-confusion shadow of an internal `@livingdesign/react` package, so any installer whose resolver picks public npm over a private registry will have host and network details shipped to the third-party Interactsh server. Whether or not this is described as bug-bounty research, the exfiltration runs against every installer without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
